rulings.Case Title: WILIAM GWIRE, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA GWIRE LAW VS. ELLIOT BLUMBERG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 16, 2015, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT WILIAM GWIRE, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA GWIRE LAW's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT The court grants the motion to strike in part. Mr. Blumberg is given leave to file a Second Amended Cross-complaint that contains ultimate facts and removes reference to irrelevant third party lawsuits that Mr. Gwire has litigated. The remainder of the motion to strike is denied. Mr. Gwire fails to show that the pleading is a sham and the court notes that the notice of motion does not fully comply with CRC 3.1322. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: WILIAM GWIRE, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA GWIRE LAW VS. ELLIOT BLUMBERG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Elliot Blumbergs Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Contempt And Or Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 16, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT ELLIOT BLUMBERG's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Contempt And Or Sanctions The motion is denied. The MOU is not a settlement that complies with CCP 664.6. The MOU expressly contemplates that the parties will enter into a subsequent "comprehensive" or "final" settlement. The court denies plaintiff's contempt request or request for an OSC regarding contempt. Mr. Blumberg fails to show that he complied with all of the conditions precedent to compel Mr. Gwire to remove thetruthaboutelliotblumberg.com website. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: WILIAM GWIRE, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA GWIRE LAW VS. ELLIOT BLUMBERG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 16, 2015, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT WILIAM GWIRE, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA GWIRE LAW's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT The demurrer is overruled. Mr. Blumberg states a cause of action for fraud/concealment and conversion. The claims are not time barred. The cross-complaint is compulsory per CCP 426.10(c) because the complaint and cross-claims arise "out of the same transaction, occurrence, or series of transactions or occurrences." While the amended cross-complaint includes extraneous material, Mr. Gwire can reasonably respond. The amended cross-complaint expressly only has two causes of action brought solely by Mr. Blumberg and demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA HAMMES et al VS. HAIGHT & FILLMORE ASSOCIATES LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Barbara Hammes' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 16, 2015, Line 1, Plaintiff Barbara Hammes' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery (Part 1 of 2 For Purposes of Entry of Tentative Ruling). Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This is an action alleging various habitability causes of action, violations of statutes, breach of contract, fraud and constructive eviction brought by two former tenants of a building which was destroyed by fire and rebuilt. Defendant Flynn is a co-owner of the building. Discovery has been exchanged and depositions of plaintiffs completed. In February, 2015 plaintiff Hammes served on defendant Flynn a request (the "Request") for the production of documents. This Request was very similar to requests made by plaintiff Hammes to defendant Haight & Fillmore, the other building co-owner, in October, 2014, and to defendant, Meridian Management Group Company, the managing agent of the building, in February, 2015. Defendants Haight and Meridian served documents in response to the requests served on them. Defendant Flynn responded to the Request on March 23, 2015. Plaintiffs intend to take the depositions of Flynn and Meridian in July and dates are to be obtained. On May 29, 2015 plaintiff served this motion (the "Motion") to compel further responses to the Request. There had been a meet and confer letter from plaintiff Hammes' counsel on April 16, 2015 to which counsel for defendant Flynn responded on May 11, 2015. On May 15, 2015 Meridian and Mr. Flynn produced additional documents intended to be responsive to plaintiff's requests, including the Request. Defendant Flynn's amended responses to the Request were served on June 11, 2015. There has been no attempt on the part of plaintiff's counsel at a further meet and confer conference since her April 16 letter. The record shows no response to defendant counsel's May 11 letter; nor any attempt by plaintiff's counsel at a meet and confer after defendants' further responses in late May and June; nor any further attempt by plaintiff's counsel at a meet and confer prior to serving the Motion. There is no need to further delve into the facts here, given the tentative ruling below. (Tentative Ruling Continued in Part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA HAMMES et al VS. HAIGHT & FILLMORE ASSOCIATES LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Barbara Hammes' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 16, 2015, Line 1, Plaintiff Barbara Hammes' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling). (Continued from Part 1 of 2) The tentative ruling is that, under the circumstances, 1. The Motion is denied without prejudice; and 2. No sanctions are ordered; and 3. By on or before July 27, 2015, counsel for plaintiff Hammes and defendant Flynn are ordered to and shall conduct a comprehensive meet and confer at least by telephone and preferably in person to reach a mutually acceptable understanding on the production of the documents requested in the Request, taking into account, inter alia, that a) there has probably been considerable duplication in the documents sought by the Request and those sought from, and produced by, defendants Haight Fillmore and Meridian Management, as well as those already produced by defendant Flynn; b) the additional documents produced by defendants in May and June need to be taken into account in the meet and confer discussions; c) there are planned depositions by plaintiff that might assist in identifying and furnishing with greater precision relevant documents to be produced; d) after an agreement as to documents to be produced relevant to the building in question, and, based on need and relevance, there might be considered an agreement for the limited production of documents with respect to other buildings owned by defendant Flynn, if in his possession and not produced by the other defendants; and e) the "relevant period" for the document production for the building by Mr, Flynn as to documents in his possession might more sensibly be limited to no more than the four year period prior to the date of the fire in the building on September 27, 2011; and 4. By on or before July 31, 2015 the agreement with respect to document production under the Request shall be reduced to writing; and 5. By on or before August 15, 2015 the agreed on document production by defendant Flynn under the Request shall have occurred prior to plaintiff's planned depositions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY D. CHU VS. LEAP TRANSIT, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Leap Transits Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 16, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JEFFREY CHU'S Motion To Compel Defendant Leap Transits Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions (Part 1 of 2 For Purposes of Entry of Tentative Ruling). Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This action is for, inter alia, damages under various causes of action arising out of plaintiff's 2014 termination by defendant Leap Transit, Inc.("Leap") as a director and employee of defendant Leap, a start up company of which he was a co-founder and shareholder. Plaintiff has moved to compel further responses to its first request (the "First Request") to Leap for the production of documents and for sanctions. The action was filed on August 29, 2014. The First Request was served on November 26, 2014. Defendant Leap's response was served on January 20, 2015, and set forth a number of objections to the First Request. Given my tentative ruling it is not necessary that I delve into the parties' assertions with respect to the First Request. Apparently, according to plaintiff, in April, 2015, there was some document production pursuant to the First Request. Then, starting on May 6, 2015, there was a first meet and confer on May 6, 2015 and a second telephone meet and confer on May 12, 2015. There are several letters and emails back and forth between the parties during mid May, 2015 confirming this and outlining ways and means to complete the production under the First Request, to the extent that, in a letter dated May 15, 2015, plaintiff's counsel stated that "it looks like we are very close to resolving all open issues." At that juncture there was no discussion or understanding as to exactly when the the production of the documents was to occur. Then, without any apparently further meet and confer efforts, plaintiff filled this motion on June 11, 2015. (Tentative Ruling Continued in Part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY D. CHU VS. LEAP TRANSIT, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL

rulings.Case Title: MOHAMED ZINABIDINE VS. KIMMI KIM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damage Alegations From Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 16, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANTS DENNIS' CHAMPION AUTO BODY & REPAIR, DENNIS KIM AND KIMBERLY KIM'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damage Alegations From Plaintiffs Complaint Defendant's motion to strike punitive damages is denied. Under CCP § 436(a) to prevail on a motion to strike, the movant must establish that the complaint contains irrelevant, false or improper allegations. A motion to strike punitive damages allegations may be granted where the claim sued upon would not support an award of punitive damages as a matter of law [See Civ.C. § 3294(a); Commodore Home Systems, Inc. v. Sup.Ct. (Brown) (1982) 32 C3d 211, 214-215.] Here, (1) Defendant has not established the complaint contains irrelevant, false or improper allegations and, (2) the allegations contained in the complaint may support punitive damages as a matter of law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.
